stimulus: Within certain deep-sea fish species, some individuals develop an enlarged swim bladder—an apparent overdevelopment of the gas-filled organ used to control buoyancy. Given the bladder's greater prevalence among fish that make rapid vertical ascents, some researchers hypothesize that its role isn't ______blank; rather, the enlarged bladder may actively assist with quick depth changes.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. "Dormant" most logically completes the text. The researchers' hypothesis is that the enlarged bladder actively assists with rapid ascents; the sentence introduces this idea by saying that the bladder's role "isn't ______"—it isn't inactive or unused. "Dormant" (inactive, unused) sets up the contrast with "actively assist."

Choice B is incorrect. "Operative" means functioning, but the hypothesis is that the bladder IS operative, not that it isn't. The negation makes "operative" the opposite of what the sentence needs. Choice C is incorrect. "Predetermined" means decided in advance and doesn't pair with "rather, the bladder may actively assist." Choice D is incorrect. "Replicable" means able to be reproduced; the text concerns whether the bladder is doing something, not whether the structure can be reproduced.
